title: Urostachys christii
intro: Urostachys christii, also known as Brazilian spike moss, is a species of epiphytic fern belonging to the family Lindsaeaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Urostachys christii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal effects. Urostachys christii is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and can also be found in capsule or powder form, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
